name: CMA-ES
key_facts: CMA-ES (Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy) is a derivative-free optimization algorithm
It adapts a full covariance matrix to guide the sampling of candidate solutions
It is commonly used for non-convex, non-separable, and ill-conditioned black-box optimization
CMA-ES was developed by Nikolaus Hansen and Andreas Ostermeier in the 1990s
The algorithm maintains and updates a mean vector, step size, and covariance matrix
It is considered state-of-the-art for continuous black-box optimization in low-to-moderate dimensions
CMA-ES is invariant to order-preserving transformations of the objective function
forbidden_claims: CMA-ES requires gradients of the objective function
CMA-ES guarantees finding the global optimum
CMA-ES only works with diagonal covariance matrices
